raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "All in-state sales of new passenger cars and trucks are required to be zero-emission by 2035.",
    "Heavy-duty vehicles must be zero-emission by 2045.",
    "The Executive Order N-79-20 remains neutral on what technologies will be used to achieve zero-emission goals.",
    "Medium- and heavy-duty trucks, buses, vessels, locomotives, and off-road equipment significantly impact local air quality and greenhouse gas emissions.",
    "Hydrogen fuel cell technology is particularly well-suited for medium- and heavy-duty vehicles due to its longer range and higher power density.",
    "Hydrogen fuel cell electric mobility solutions have been largely deprioritized compared to battery-based vehicles in California's clean transportation investments.",
    "Battery-electric vehicles alone cannot achieve California's zero-emission goals, and fuel cell electric vehicles need to be included in the portfolio.",
    "There will be no single solution or silver bullet for achieving zero-emission mobility."
  ]
}
```
